body.title: Write root ARCHITECTURE.md + per-device docs/ pattern
body.result: root ARCHITECTURE.md (nav map) + devices/inference/docs/ + devices/librarian/docs/; project→device→file navigation chain established
body.description:
Establish the two-level architecture doc pattern: root ARCHITECTURE.md is a minimal map (fewest possible tokens) that names the components and points to their device folders. Each device folder gets devices/{device}/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md that describes that device's internals and points to specific files. Navigation path: root → device folder → ARCHITECTURE.md → specific file → ready to edit.

Root ARCHITECTURE.md shape: component table (device, folder, arch doc link) + storage schema map + one-line platform description. Under 50 lines total — it's a pointer document, not content.

Device ARCHITECTURE.md shape: what this device does (2-3 sentences), key files with one-line descriptions, external interfaces (comms://, MCP tools exposed, env vars required), related devices. Under 80 lines.

Ship the root doc + docs/ for inference and librarian (the two active devices). devices/igor/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md comes in T-igor-into-adc-device.

**Affected files:** ARCHITECTURE.md (root, new), devices/inference/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (new), devices/librarian/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (new)
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code — architecture doc is adjacent to the code it describes. No separate docs repo.
**Scope boundary:** inference and librarian only. Platform/skeleton arch doc is a follow-on. Igor's arch doc comes with the migration ticket.
**Test plan:** no automated test. Manual: root doc links resolve, device docs name the correct files, a new reader could navigate from root to a specific function in under 3 hops.
